rulings.Case Title: SIMA ZOKAEI VS. SALEM MANSOIR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment In Conformance Therewith
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 26, 2023, line 1. Petitioner's Motion to Confirm Arbitration Award and Enter a judgment is continued to February 23, 2023 to be heard with other pending motion. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 26, 2023, line 2. Intervenor's Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement is DENIED. The relief requested in this motion is not available under the MOU. The provisions that the moving party seeks to enforce (e.g. dismissal, un-consolidation, interpleader) are all expressly dependent on the execution of a settlement agreement (unlike other provisions that are enforceable upon the execution of the MOU). It appears from the response filed by Nationstar that this party is willing to interplead the amounts discussed in the MOU notwithstanding any evidence of submission of W-9 or execution of the settlement agreement, Nationstar may make a motion seeking such relief. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: GUSTAVE LINK VS. NOOR ISSA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 26, 2023, line 5. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend as to the causes of action for negligence, breach of warranty of habitability (tort and contract) with leave to amend to allege in good faith a landlord-tenant relationship between Plaintiff and the moving defendant and to expressly allege inconsistencies (sham pleading doctrine) between the allegations in the verified complaint and the First Amended Complaint. Demurrer is sustained as to all other causes of action. No leave of Court was obtained to add those causes of action. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: NORTHRIDGE COOPERATIVE HOMES, INC. VS. MANCONIA GREEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 26, 2023, line 10. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to clearly allege in the Complaint whether TPA 2019 applies and whether it was complied with (potentially as an alternative allegation). Plaintiff chose to use a Form Complaint, which does not always fit circumstances of each individual cases. (e.g. Form complaint directs that paragraph 8 should only be completed if paragraph 7(b) is checked). Plaintiff may consider not using a Form Complaint. Furthermore, Plaintiff must reconcile allegations in the Notice (asserting that Defendant is in violation of SFRO) with implied allegation (confirmed by argument in opposition) that SFRO does not apply to this tenancy. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that inclusion of potentially non-applicable/inconsistent grounds for eviction in the Notice renders the Notice defective. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)
